baseVersion: v2 complete as of S160 on branch ralph/luminary-v2

v3Philosophy.theme: Notes become first-class knowledge citizens: organized into typed collections, connected to the entity graph, and discoverable through semantic clustering and hierarchical tags.
principles: Notes are graph nodes, not flat records. Every note saved fires an async task that upserts a Kuzu NOTE node and edges to Entity nodes via GLiNER extraction. The graph is the connective tissue; SQL is the authoritative store.

Hierarchical tags with a controlled vocabulary. Tags follow a slash-convention (science/biology/genetics). A shadow index table (NoteTagIndexModel) makes prefix-search O(1) and enables hierarchy-aware filtering. Tag normalization via embedding similarity prevents vocabulary explosion.

Collections replace group_name. Notes belong to zero or many named collections (many-to-many). Collections have metadata (description, color) and support 2-level nesting. Semantic clustering (HDBSCAN) auto-suggests collections from embedding similarity -- human approves, machine proposes.

Performance contracts are explicit. The 1024-dim vector schema must match the embedding model (bge-m3). Tag index queries must pass p95 < 100ms at 1,000 notes. Every new async path follows the established asyncio.create_task fire-and-forget pattern with explicit error logging.

Cross-tab navigation is event-driven. Clicking a tag node in the Viz tab fires a 'luminary:navigate' DOM event; App.tsx routes to Notes tab with the tag pre-applied. No URL hacks, no shared global state beyond Zustand store.
Every new service method and every new API endpoint must have at least one pytest test. No exceptions (inherited from v2 philosophy).

Phase 2 seed (post Phase 1): note-to-note bidirectional links (S171), notes visible in Knowledge graph (S172), collection health reports (S173), export to Obsidian vault and Anki (S174), multi-document notes (S175). Phase 2 gate cleared 2026-03-26: docs/v3-phase1-completion.md fully checked, all S161-S170 pass=true.

Phase 3 (UX Polish and Learner Focus, S176-S183): ruthlessly separates the learner layer from the diagnostic/admin layer. Notes get a reader-first layout with clickable tag navigation (S176). Monitoring becomes a Progress tab with all dev metrics hidden at /admin (S177). Study tab declutters with Smart Generate and merged health panels (S178). Flashcard generation becomes context-aware and best-practices-driven (S179). Chat settings move to a drawer with collapsed transparency panels (S180). Viz gets a collapsible View Options panel, select-all entity controls, and hides Call Graph for non-code documents (S181). YouTube transcripts gain full Read section parity (S182). Learning tab LibraryOverview slims to a single stats bar with document list as primary focus (S183).

Phase 4 (Learner Experience Refinement, S184-S190): eliminates friction in the three highest-touch surfaces -- Study, Chat, and Notes. Flashcard navigation drops the deck concept in favour of a unified search-and-filter browser (S184). Study tab collapses to two CTAs with an Insights accordion (S185). Chat brings document scope back inline and removes confusion-signal noise (S186-S187). Flashcard generation gets deeper context and Bloom L3+ bias (S188). Auto-organize shows a holistic plan instead of individual suggestion cards (S189). Tag tree gains type-ahead search (S190).

stories.title: Note collections: schema, API, and group_name migration
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: NoteModel.group_name is a freeform string giving each note exactly one optional group label with no metadata, no nesting, and no many-to-many support. As notes grow past a few dozen, users need named collections with descriptions and colors, the ability to assign one note to multiple collections, and optional parent-child nesting (e.g., Learning > Physics > Quantum). group_name is deprecated in this story but kept as a nullable column for backward compatibility. USER JOURNEY: User has 80 notes scattered across 12 distinct group_name values -> opens Notes tab -> sees new 'Collections' sidebar section -> clicks 'Create collection' -> creates 'Quantum Mechanics' under parent collection 'Physics' -> assigns existing notes via checkbox panel -> sidebar shows 'Physics (45)' expandable to 'Quantum Mechanics (18)'. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: None from prior Phase 13 stories. NoteModel.group_name (S87 NoteEditorDialog) preserved as nullable -- do not remove the column. GET /notes groups endpoint (S89) remains functional; add collection_id filter alongside. BACKEND: NoteCollectionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), name (str required), description (str nullable), color (str hex default '#6366F1'), icon (str nullable, emoji), parent_collection_id (FK to self nullable), sort_order (int default 0), created_at, updated_at. NoteCollectionMemberModel pivot: note_id (cascade delete), collection_id (cascade delete), added_at, composite PK (note_id, collection_id). db_init.py migration: CREATE TABLE note_collections; CREATE TABLE note_collection_members; SELECT DISTINCT group_name FROM notes WHERE group_name IS NOT NULL; INSERT INTO note_collections(id, name, color) for each distinct value using a generated UUID; INSERT INTO note_collection_members SELECT id, <new_collection_id> FROM notes WHERE group_name = <value>. Router routers/collections.py (new file): GET /collections (flat list with note_count), GET /collections/tree (nested JSON, children array), POST /collections, PUT /collections/{id}, DELETE /collections/{id} (cascades members, not notes), POST /collections/{id}/notes (add note, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), DELETE /collections/{id}/notes/{note_id}. Nesting constraint: POST /collections returns 422 when the requested parent_collection_id itself has a parent (max depth = 2). GET /notes gains optional collection_id query param: adds JOIN note_collection_members WHERE collection_id = :cid. Register router in main.py. CollectionSchema TypedDict: id, name, description, color, icon, parent_collection_id, sort_order, note_count, children (tree endpoint only). TESTS: Unit test: collection at depth 3 returns 422. Unit test: DELETE /collections/{id} cascades pivot rows; notes survive. Unit test: migration inserts one collection per distinct group_name with correct membership. Unit test: GET /notes?collection_id={id} returns only notes in that collection via pivot join.

stories.title: Hierarchical tags: shadow index table, canonical tag registry, prefix-search API
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Tags are stored as flat JSON arrays in notes.tags with no canonical registry, no hierarchy, and no SQL index -- tag filtering via json_each(notes.tags) is O(n) over the full notes table. PKM research on 8,000-note vaults confirms flat tag systems break at scale: synonym explosion ('ml', 'machine-learning', 'Machine Learning') and lack of browse hierarchy (clicking 'science' should match 'science/biology'). This story adds: (1) NoteTagIndexModel -- a shadow/denorm table that explodes each note's tags into indexed rows for O(1) lookup; (2) CanonicalTagModel -- a registry supporting slash-convention parent-child hierarchy ('science/biology/genetics'); (3) a full tag API including prefix-search autocomplete; (4) hierarchical matching in GET /notes?tag= (query for 'science' also returns 'science/biology' and 'science/physics/quantum' notes). USER JOURNEY: User types 'prog' in tag filter -> autocomplete shows 'programming', 'programming/python', 'programming/go' -> clicks 'programming' -> all programming/* notes shown in main pane. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: GET /notes tag filter (S89) rewritten to use NoteTagIndexModel prefix query. Note create/update/delete routes (S89) must call _sync_tag_index synchronously. The existing suggest-tags endpoint (S89) returns flat strings; S162 registers them into CanonicalTagModel on upsert. BACKEND: NoteTagIndexModel in models.py: note_id (str, no FK -- shadow table to avoid FK overhead), tag_full (str e.g. 'science/biology'), tag_root (str first segment 'science'), tag_parent (str all-but-last segment 'science', empty string if top-level). Unique composite on (note_id, tag_full). CanonicalTagModel: id = tag_full slug (PK), display_name (str), parent_tag (str nullable, FK to self), note_count (int denorm), created_at. TagAliasModel: alias (str PK), canonical_tag_id (FK). Sync function _sync_tag_index(note_id, tags, db) in routers/notes.py: called synchronously on note create/update/delete. On create/update: DELETE FROM note_tag_index WHERE note_id = :nid; INSERT for each tag in tags; UPDATE CanonicalTagModel note_count (+1 for new, -1 for removed). On delete: DELETE all rows for note_id; decrement counts. NOTE: _sync_tag_index is called synchronously within the same DB write transaction -- do NOT use asyncio.create_task here. Use Semaphore(1) if concurrent note writes are ever added. Router routers/tags.py (new file): GET /tags (flat list sorted note_count DESC, fields: id, display_name, note_count, parent_tag), GET /tags/tree (nested with children), GET /tags/{tag_id}/notes (notes with this tag including children), GET /tags/autocomplete?q= (LIKE :q || '%' on NoteTagIndexModel.tag_full, up to 10 results), POST /tags (create canonical), PUT /tags/{tag_id} (rename display_name or re-parent), DELETE /tags/{tag_id} (409 if note_count > 0). GET /notes?tag= rewritten: WHERE note_id IN (SELECT note_id FROM note_tag_index WHERE tag_full = :tag OR tag_full LIKE :tag || '/%'). Register router in main.py. db_init.py migration: CREATE TABLE note_tag_index; CREATE TABLE canonical_tags; CREATE TABLE tag_aliases; CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_tag_index_tag_full ON note_tag_index(tag_full); CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_tag_index_note_id ON note_tag_index(note_id); backfill: SELECT id, tags FROM notes; call _sync logic in pure SQL for existing rows. TESTS: Unit test: note with tags ['programming/python', 'programming/go'] produces 2 NoteTagIndexModel rows with tag_root='programming'. Unit test: GET /notes?tag=programming returns notes tagged 'programming', 'programming/python', 'programming/go'. Unit test: deleting a note removes its NoteTagIndexModel rows and decrements CanonicalTagModel.note_count. Unit test: autocomplete for 'prog' returns 'programming' and its children sorted by note_count.

stories.title: Notes as Kuzu graph nodes: NOTE type, WRITTEN_ABOUT entity edges, TAG_IS_CONCEPT bridge
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Notes exist entirely outside the Kuzu knowledge graph. The only bridge is the transient chat notes_node which retrieves note text via NoteSearchService at query time -- there are no persistent graph edges from notes to entities. This means the Viz knowledge graph shows no notes, note discovery through graph traversal is impossible, and queries like 'find all notes mentioning entities related to Gradient Descent' require a full-text scan instead of a graph traversal. This story adds three structural additions to Kuzu: NOTE node type, WRITTEN_ABOUT edges (NOTE -> Entity, auto-extracted via GLiNER on note save), and TAG_IS_CONCEPT edges (NOTE -> Entity, when a tag matches an existing Entity.name). DERIVED_FROM edges (NOTE -> Document) link notes to source books. USER JOURNEY: User saves a note about 'backpropagation' while reading a deep learning book -> async background task runs GLiNER, finds existing Entity 'Backpropagation' in Kuzu, creates WRITTEN_ABOUT edge; tag 'neural-networks' matches Entity 'neural-networks', creates TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge -> user opens Viz tab -> note node appears connected to both entities. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: POST /notes, PUT /notes/{id}, DELETE /notes/{id} (S89) each gain an asyncio.create_task call (upsert or delete). chat_graph.py notes_node (S79) upgraded: after NoteSearchService.search(), enriches context snippets with Kuzu entity names via get_entities_for_note(). Kuzu schema adds 3 edge tables and 1 node table to existing graph (S77 introduced the schema). BACKEND: graph.py _create_schema() additions: NODE TABLE Note(id STRING, note_id STRING, preview STRING, created_at STRING) PRIMARY KEY(id). REL TABLE WRITTEN_ABOUT(FROM Note TO Entity) {confidence: FLOAT}. REL TABLE TAG_IS_CONCEPT(FROM Note TO Entity) {tag: STRING}. REL TABLE DERIVED_FROM(FROM Note TO Document). NoteGraphService in app/services/note_graph.py: async upsert_note_node(note_id, content, document_id, tags, db): (1) Upsert Note node (id=note_id, preview=content[:200], created_at=now ISO string). (2) If document_id: upsert DERIVED_FROM edge to Document node. (3) Run GLiNER on content for PERSON, ORGANIZATION, CONCEPT, TECHNOLOGY entity types. For each extracted entity: run Kuzu query to look up Entity by name (case-insensitive LOWER() comparison); guard with has_next() before get_next() -- do not call get_next() without has_next() check (Kuzu raises on empty result). If found: upsert WRITTEN_ABOUT edge with confidence = entity_score. (4) For each tag: Kuzu lookup Entity where LOWER(name) = LOWER(tag); if found: upsert TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge. Called from notes router as asyncio.create_task (fire-and-forget, same pattern as vector embeddings). Wrap the task body in try/except and log errors via app logger -- fire-and-forget must never fail silently. async delete_note_node(note_id): DELETE FROM Note WHERE note_id = :nid (Kuzu cascades incident edges on node delete). Called synchronously in DELETE /notes/{id} handler before note row deletion. async get_entities_for_note(note_id): Cypher MATCH (n:Note)-[r:WRITTEN_ABOUT|TAG_IS_CONCEPT]->(e:Entity) WHERE n.note_id = $nid RETURN e.name, e.type, r.confidence, type(r). Guard each get_next() with has_next(). async get_notes_for_entity(entity_name): MATCH (n:Note)-[:WRITTEN_ABOUT|:TAG_IS_CONCEPT]->(e:Entity) WHERE LOWER(e.name) = LOWER($name) RETURN n.note_id. GET /notes/{note_id}/entities: calls get_entities_for_note, returns [{name, type, confidence, edge_type}]. chat_graph.py notes_node: after NoteSearchService results, for top-3 results call get_entities_for_note(note_id) and append entity names to context chunk metadata. TESTS: Unit test: upsert_note_node for note whose content matches an existing Entity creates WRITTEN_ABOUT edge; entity absent from Kuzu is skipped without error. Unit test: note with document_id creates DERIVED_FROM edge to correct Document node. Unit test: tag matching existing Entity.name creates TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge. Unit test: delete_note_node removes Note node; subsequent get_entities_for_note returns []. Unit test: get_notes_for_entity returns correct note_id for a known entity.
acceptanceCriteria: Kuzu schema in graph.py: Note node table (id, note_id, preview, created_at), WRITTEN_ABOUT rel (Note->Entity, confidence FLOAT), TAG_IS_CONCEPT rel (Note->Entity, tag STRING), DERIVED_FROM rel (Note->Document)

stories.title: Notes performance refactor: fix vector dimension mismatch (384->1024), add tag indexes, batch reindex
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Three technical debts in the notes subsystem must be resolved before Phase 1 work scales to large note corpora. (1) VECTOR DIM BUG: The LanceDB note_vectors_v2 table schema is defined with pa.list_(pa.float32(), 384) in vector_store.py but BAAI/bge-m3 produces 1024-dim embeddings -- a silent mismatch that causes vector upserts to fail or produce truncated vectors, making semantic note search unreliable. (2) TAG QUERY INDEXES: S162 added NoteTagIndexModel with indexes in db_init.py. This story adds a @pytest.mark.slow performance regression guard to verify those indexes hold at 1,000-note scale. (3) ORPHANED EMBEDDINGS: Notes created before the vector store was initialized, or during Ollama downtime, have no embedding; there is no way to identify or backfill them without a dedicated endpoint. USER JOURNEY: Admin (developer) calls POST /admin/notes/reindex -> {queued: True, total_notes: 247} -> background task re-embeds 31 notes with missing or mismatched vectors -> GET /notes/search now returns semantically relevant results for all notes. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: vector_store.py (S89) is modified -- existing notes may have 384-dim vectors that will be erased by the migration; re-embedding all notes is required. NoteTagIndexModel indexes (S162) are verified under load here. BACKEND: NOTE_VECTOR_DIM = 1024 constant in vector_store.py. _get_or_create_note_table(): inspect existing LanceDB table schema (table.schema, check the vector field list_size via PyArrow field type). If list_size != 1024: log warning 'note_vectors_v2 schema mismatch (found {actual_dim}-dim) -- dropping and recreating with 1024-dim', call tbl.drop() (or the LanceDB equivalent for table deletion -- use db.drop_table('note_vectors_v2') via the lancedb connection object), then recreate. This detection runs at startup (called from get_vector_store()). ReindexService in app/services/reindex_service.py: async reindex_notes(db: AsyncSession) -> ReindexReport TypedDict {total: int, reindexed: int, failed: int}: fetch all NoteModel rows (id, content). For each: check if note_id present in LanceDB (synchronous tbl.search(query_vector).where(f"note_id = '{nid}'").limit(1).to_pandas() -- wrap sync call in asyncio.to_thread). If absent: re-embed content and upsert. Catch embedding exceptions per-note; increment failed. Return report. Run entire reindex as asyncio.create_task. POST /admin/notes/reindex: X-Admin-Key header checked against settings.ADMIN_KEY (env var, default empty string = no auth for local dev). If ADMIN_KEY non-empty and header missing or wrong: return 403. Returns {queued: True, total_notes: int}. PERFORMANCE TEST (@pytest.mark.slow, guarded by LUMINARY_PERF_TESTS env var per patterns.md): insert 1,000 synthetic NoteModel + NoteTagIndexModel rows via fixture. Run GET /notes?tag=science 10 times via test client. Assert p95 < 100ms (use time.perf_counter). TESTS: Unit test: _get_or_create_note_table with a mock 384-dim schema triggers drop-and-recreate warning. Unit test: reindex_notes identifies notes absent from LanceDB and returns correct counts in ReindexReport. Unit test: POST /admin/notes/reindex with wrong X-Admin-Key returns 403 when ADMIN_KEY is set to a non-empty value.

stories.title: Collections UI: sidebar tree, CRUD dialog, multi-collection assignment in note editor
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: S161 adds the backend collections system. This story builds the React frontend: a collapsible collection tree in the Notes sidebar replacing the flat group_name filter list, a create/edit/delete collection dialog, and multi-collection assignment checkboxes in NoteEditorDialog. The UX follows Obsidian's folder panel pattern (collapsible items, count badges, icon-triggered actions) within Luminary's existing shadcn/ui + Tailwind conventions. Notes can belong to multiple collections simultaneously. USER JOURNEY: User opens Notes tab -> sidebar shows 'Collections' header with '+' button -> 3 top-level collections with children -> user clicks 'Physics' to expand -> sees 'Quantum Mechanics (18)' and 'Thermodynamics (27)' -> clicks 'Quantum Mechanics' -> main pane filters to 18 notes -> clicks a note pencil icon -> NoteEditorDialog opens with 'Collections' section showing checkbox list -> user checks 'Study Targets' -> note now in both collections without clicking Save (collection membership saves immediately on check). CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteEditorDialog (S87) gains a new 'Collections' section below the existing tags section. GET /notes/{note_id} (S89) must return collection_ids array -- add this field to the note response schema. Notes sidebar 'Groups' section (S89) replaced by 'Collections'. group_name is deprecated: existing NoteEditorDialog group_name input (S87) should be removed. FRONTEND: CollectionTree component (frontend/src/components/CollectionTree.tsx): renders GET /collections/tree. Each CollectionTreeItem: colored square dot (collection.color), chevron if children present, name, note_count pill. Click = setActiveCollectionId in useAppStore; Notes page refetches with ?collection_id=. Expand/collapse via local useState. Rename: inline input on double-click or pencil icon; PUT /collections/{id} on Enter or blur. Delete: trash icon opens confirm AlertDialog ('Delete collection? Notes are not deleted.'); DELETE /collections/{id} on confirm. CreateCollectionDialog (frontend/src/components/CreateCollectionDialog.tsx): name input (required), description textarea, 8-swatch color picker (hex values), parent collection select (top-level only from GET /collections flat). POST /collections on save. Notes sidebar: replace 'Groups' section with 'Collections' label + CollectionTree + 'New Collection' button at bottom. NoteEditorDialog: add 'Collections' section below tags. Renders scrollable max-h-40 list of all collections (GET /collections flat). Checkbox per collection; pre-checked based on note's collection_ids (add collection_ids field to note response). On check: POST /collections/{id}/notes immediately. On uncheck: DELETE /collections/{id}/notes/{note_id} immediately. No save button for collection changes. Notes page useQuery: passes collection_id param when activeCollectionId set. HTML5 drag-and-drop on note table rows: onDragStart stores note_id; CollectionTree items accept onDragOver/onDrop; drop fires POST /collections/{id}/notes. No DnD library needed. stories.title: Tag co-occurrence graph in Viz tab: Sigma.js tag network mode with cross-tab note filter
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: The Viz tab renders the book entity graph via Sigma.js v3 + Graphology but shows no note-derived structure. This story adds a 'Tags' graph mode: nodes are canonical tags (sized by note_count, colored by parent tag family), edges are co-occurrences (two tags appearing on the same note, weighted by co-occurrence count). The graph reveals how the user's personal knowledge base clusters -- distinct from the entity graph which reflects book content. Clicking a tag node fires a cross-tab navigation event that activates the Notes tab with that tag applied as a filter. The graph is generated from NoteTagIndexModel (S162) with a cap of 200 nodes and 500 edges for rendering clarity. USER JOURNEY: User opens Viz tab -> clicks 'Tags' mode -> tag network renders: large 'machine-learning' node connected to 'neural-networks', 'optimization', 'backpropagation'; separate cluster 'history' -> 'ancient-rome', 'empire' -> user clicks 'machine-learning' -> Notes tab activates with tag filter applied, showing all ml/* notes. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Viz tab mode selector (S112 added entity graph modes) gains a new 'Tags' button. S162 NoteTagIndexModel is the data source. Notes page activeTag (S165 useAppStore field) is the cross-tab target. BACKEND: TagGraphService in app/services/tag_graph.py: async build_tag_graph(db: AsyncSession) -> TagGraphData TypedDict {nodes: list[TagNode], edges: list[TagEdge], generated_at: str}. TagNode: {id, label, note_count, parent_tag, color}. TagEdge: {source, target, weight}. Implementation: (1) Fetch top-200 CanonicalTagModel by note_count where note_count > 0. (2) Co-occurrence SQL: SELECT a.tag_full, b.tag_full, COUNT(*) as weight FROM note_tag_index a JOIN note_tag_index b ON a.note_id = b.note_id AND a.tag_full < b.tag_full WHERE a.tag_full IN (:top_200) AND b.tag_full IN (:top_200) GROUP BY 1, 2 HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 500. (3) Node color: deterministic from parent_tag -- hash parent_tag string to index 0-11, map to 12-color palette (hex constants in service). Nodes with no parent use a default grey. In-memory LRU invalidation: TagGraphService caches the result dict; cache is cleared in _sync_tag_index whenever any tag row is modified. GET /tags/graph: calls build_tag_graph; returns TagGraphData. TypedDicts in app/types/. FRONTEND: Viz tab: add 'Tags' button to mode selector. TagGraph component (frontend/src/components/viz/TagGraph.tsx): import Sigma + Graphology (same pattern as existing VizGraph). Build Graphology graph: nodes with size = Math.max(10, Math.sqrt(noteCount) * 6), edges with weight. Apply ForceAtlas2 layout (same settings as existing graph). On Sigma node click: dispatch new CustomEvent('luminary:navigate', { detail: { tab: 'notes', tagFilter: nodeId } }) -- the tagFilter field maps to the activeTag field in useAppStore. App.tsx useEffect: listen for 'luminary:navigate'; on fire, setActiveTab to 'notes' and call setActiveTag(tagFilter) in useAppStore. Notes page: on mount, read activeTag from store and pre-populate tag filter (same mechanism as S165 activeTag). STATES: TagGraph loading: centered Spinner. Empty (< 3 unique tags): 'Take more notes to see your tag network' message. Error: inline 'Could not load tag graph' with retry button. TESTS: Unit test: build_tag_graph with 4-note fixture sharing known tag pairs returns correct edge weights. Unit test: TagGraphService cache is cleared after _sync_tag_index modifies a tag row. Vitest: TagGraph renders correct node count from mock GET /tags/graph fixture. Vitest: node click dispatches luminary:navigate event with correct tagFilter value.

stories.title: Smart tag normalization: embedding similarity scan, merge suggestions, accept/reject drawer
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Even with hierarchical tags (S162), users accumulate synonymous tags across notes from different books and sessions: 'ml', 'machine-learning', 'Machine Learning', 'AI', 'artificial-intelligence'. The LLM tagger (S89) generates new tags per-note without checking the existing controlled vocabulary. PKM research on 8,000-note vaults found 2,141 unique tags -- roughly 1 per 4 notes -- an unmanageable vocabulary that defeats the purpose of tagging. This story adds a SmartTagNormalizerService that embeds all canonical tag names using bge-m3, finds semantically similar pairs above a 0.85 cosine similarity threshold, and surfaces merge suggestions in a NormalizationDrawer where the user reviews and accepts or rejects each pair. USER JOURNEY: User clicks 'Normalize' (wrench icon) in Notes sidebar Tags section -> scan runs -> drawer opens: 6 suggestions including 'ml -- machine-learning (91% similar, 34 notes)' -> user accepts 4, rejects 2 -> 62 notes updated with canonical tags. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: POST /tags/merge (S165) is reused by the accept action. TagAliasModel and CanonicalTagModel (S162) are the target schema. _sync_tag_index (S162) is called by merge for each updated note. The NoteTagIndexModel cache invalidation (S167 TagGraphService) must be triggered by the merge operation. BACKEND: TagMergeSuggestionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), tag_a_id (str, FK CanonicalTagModel.id), tag_b_id (str), similarity (float), suggested_canonical_id (str -- whichever of the pair has higher note_count), status ('pending'|'accepted'|'rejected'), created_at. db_init.py: CREATE TABLE tag_merge_suggestions. SmartTagNormalizerService in app/services/tag_normalizer.py: async scan(db) -> int: fetch all CanonicalTagModel rows. Embed display_names using the same embedding model as the retriever -- call the embeddings function used in NoteSearchService (e.g. embed a list of strings via the ONNX bge-m3 session, same path as chunk/note embedding). Uses the same bge-m3 ONNX model as the retriever (1024-dim, requires S170 to have run first to ensure note_vectors_v2 is 1024-dim). Call asyncio.to_thread for embedding calls. Compute pairwise cosine similarity as numpy dot product on L2-normalised vectors. For pairs with similarity > 0.85 where neither is an alias of the other (check TagAliasModel): insert TagMergeSuggestionModel row; suggested_canonical = whichever has higher note_count. Return count of new suggestions created. POST /tags/normalization/scan: triggers scan as asyncio.create_task; returns {queued: True}. GET /tags/normalization/suggestions: returns pending TagMergeSuggestionModel rows, each with tag_a {id, display_name, note_count} and tag_b {id, display_name, note_count} expanded. POST /tags/normalization/suggestions/{id}/accept: calls existing POST /tags/merge logic (reuse, not duplicate) with tag_a_id and suggested_canonical_id; sets status=accepted; returns {affected_notes: int}. POST /tags/normalization/suggestions/{id}/reject: sets status=rejected. FRONTEND: Tags section header in Notes sidebar: 'Normalize' wrench icon button. On click: POST /tags/normalization/scan; show spinner; poll GET /tags/normalization/suggestions every 2s until suggestions arrive; open NormalizationDrawer (Sheet from right). NormalizationDrawer: each suggestion row shows TagA chip -- TagB chip, similarity badge (e.g. '91%'), '{n} notes affected'. Accept button (CheckIcon): calls POST /suggestions/{id}/accept; row transitions to green 'Merged -- {n} notes updated'. Reject button (XIcon): row fades out. stories.title: Semantic note clustering via HDBSCAN: auto-suggest collections from embedding similarity
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Note collections (S161) require manual curation -- users must create collections and assign notes themselves. At scale this is a maintenance burden. PKM research confirms that at 1,000+ notes automated organization is non-negotiable. This story adds a semantic clustering service using HDBSCAN (Hierarchical Density-Based Spatial Clustering) over note embeddings from LanceDB note_vectors_v2, generates collection name suggestions via LLM, and surfaces them in a 'Suggested Collections' sidebar panel. HDBSCAN is preferred over k-means because it handles variable cluster sizes, does not require pre-specifying cluster count, and marks noise notes (label=-1) as unclustered rather than forcing them into a group. The note_vectors_v2 table now uses 1024-dim vectors (S170 fixed the schema) so clustering produces accurate semantic groups. USER JOURNEY: User has 120 notes with minimal manual organization -> clicks 'Auto-organize' in Notes sidebar -> system clusters notes -> 7 suggested collections: 'Quantum Mechanics (18 notes)', 'Machine Learning (34 notes)', 'Ancient History (12 notes)' etc. -> user reviews each with 3-note preview -> accepts 5 -> 5 new NoteCollections created with notes assigned. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Depends on S161 (NoteCollectionModel + NoteCollectionMemberModel created by accept). Depends on S170 (1024-dim note_vectors_v2 -- clustering only runs if vectors exist). S164 CollectionTree must refresh after accept to show new collections. BACKEND: scikit-learn >= 1.3 in pyproject.toml (uses sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN added in scikit-learn 1.3 -- no extra hdbscan package needed). ClusterSuggestionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), suggested_name (str), note_ids (JSON list of str), confidence_score (float, mean pairwise cosine within cluster), status ('pending'|'accepted'|'rejected'), created_at. db_init.py: CREATE TABLE cluster_suggestions. ClusteringService in app/services/clustering_service.py: async cluster_notes(db) -> int: (1) Fetch all rows from LanceDB note_vectors_v2 via await asyncio.to_thread(table.to_pandas); extract note_id and vector columns. (2) Build numpy matrix from vectors. If fewer than 6 notes with vectors: return 0 (not enough data). (3) Run sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=3, min_samples=2, metric='cosine').fit_predict(matrix). (4) For each label != -1: gather member note_ids; compute centroid; select up to 3 medoid notes (closest to centroid by cosine distance). (5) Compute confidence_score = mean pairwise cosine similarity for cluster members. (6) LLM name generation: build prompt with up to 3 note excerpts (content[:150] each). LiteLLM call: system='Given these note excerpts, suggest a 2-4 word collection name capturing their common theme. Reply with the name only.'; user=excerpts. (7) Insert ClusterSuggestionModel row. Return count of new suggestions. Rate-limit: if a ClusterSuggestionModel with status=pending exists and created_at > now - 1 hour: skip clustering; return -1. POST /notes/cluster: if rate-limited (returns -1): return {cached: True, last_run: datetime ISO str}. Else triggers cluster_notes as asyncio.create_task; returns {queued: True, total_notes: int}. GET /notes/cluster/suggestions: returns pending ClusterSuggestionModel rows sorted by confidence_score DESC; each row includes first 3 note content previews (first 100 chars of each note content fetched by note_ids). POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/{id}/accept: create NoteCollectionModel(name=suggested_name, color='#6366F1'), bulk INSERT NoteCollectionMemberModel for all note_ids, set status=accepted; return {collection_id}. POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/{id}/reject: set status=rejected. FRONTEND: Notes sidebar: 'Suggested Collections' section visible only when GET /notes/cluster/suggestions returns pending items. 'Auto-organize' button (Wand2 icon) at top of Collections section triggers POST /notes/cluster; shows spinner while queued. Each suggestion card: suggested name, '{n} notes' badge, confidence bar (filled based on confidence_score), 3 note excerpt previews as muted text. Accept (Plus icon) and Reject (X icon) buttons. On accept: POST /suggestions/{id}/accept; invalidate GET /collections/tree; suggestion card animates out. STATES: Scanning: spinner on Auto-organize button. No suggestions: 'Auto-organize' button remains visible with no panel below. Accepted suggestion: collection appears in CollectionTree (cache invalidation). TESTS: Unit test: HDBSCAN with 6-vector fixture (3 closely similar + 3 dissimilar) produces exactly 1 cluster with correct member note_ids. Unit test: noise notes (label=-1) excluded from ClusterSuggestionModel. Unit test: accept creates NoteCollection with correct note_ids as NoteCollectionMember rows. stories.title: Note-to-note bidirectional links: explicit Zettelkasten-style connections
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: Phase 1 connects notes to entities (WRITTEN_ABOUT) and to documents (DERIVED_FROM) in Kuzu, but notes cannot reference each other directly. Zettelkasten research shows that explicit note-to-note links -- where each link carries a typed relationship -- are the primary driver of emergent knowledge structure at scale. Without them, related notes can only be discovered via shared tags or shared entities; the user cannot express 'this note elaborates on that note', 'this note contradicts that note', or 'see also'. This story adds a NoteLink model and a LINKS_TO Kuzu edge type, a link-creation UI in the note detail view, and bidirectional backlink display in NoteEditorDialog. USER JOURNEY: User is editing a note on 'Backpropagation' -> types '[[' to trigger note link autocomplete -> types 'grad' -> dropdown shows 'Gradient Descent' and 'Gradient Tape' notes -> selects 'Gradient Descent' with link type 'elaborates' -> the note gains an outgoing link chip 'elaborates -> Gradient Descent' -> Gradient Descent note's editor now shows a backlink 'backlinked by: Backpropagation (elaborates)'. stories.title: Collection health report: cohesion score, orphaned notes, coverage gaps, stale notes
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: Collections (S161) organize notes into named groups, but there is no feedback loop telling the user whether a collection is coherent, growing stale, or missing flashcard coverage. The Deck Health Report (S160) provides this for flashcard decks; this story provides the equivalent for note collections. A low-cohesion collection (notes have very different embedding vectors) signals it needs splitting. Orphaned notes (in no collection) signal organizational debt. Notes with no linked flashcards signal study coverage gaps. USER JOURNEY: User opens Notes tab -> clicks gear icon on 'Machine Learning' collection -> Collection Health panel opens: Cohesion 78% (green), 3 orphaned notes in library, 5 notes with no flashcard coverage, 2 notes not edited in 90+ days (stale) -> user clicks 'Move uncovered notes to study queue' -> GenerateFlashcardsDialog pre-populated with those 5 notes -> user generates 10 new cards. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteCollectionModel (S161) is the target entity. FlashcardModel.source_content_hash (S169) used to identify notes already covered. Note embeddings from note_vectors_v2 (S170 dim fix) used for cohesion score. BACKEND: CollectionHealthService in app/services/collection_health.py. CollectionHealthReport TypedDict: {collection_id, collection_name, cohesion_score: float, note_count: int, orphaned_notes: list[str], uncovered_notes: list[{note_id, preview}], stale_notes: list[{note_id, preview, last_updated}], hotspot_tags: list[{tag, count}]}. cohesion_score: fetch vectors for all note_ids in collection from LanceDB (asyncio.to_thread), compute mean pairwise cosine similarity, return as 0-1 float. If < 6 notes: return None (insufficient data). orphaned_notes: SELECT id FROM notes WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT note_id FROM note_collection_members) -- notes belonging to no collection at all (not just this collection). uncovered_notes: notes in this collection whose id has no FlashcardModel row with source='note' and deck=collection.name. stale_notes: notes in this collection with updated_at < now - 90 days. hotspot_tags: top 5 tags by count across notes in this collection. GET /collections/{id}/health: returns CollectionHealthReport. POST /collections/{id}/health/archive-stale: sets a new NoteModel.archived (bool, nullable) column = True for stale notes; returns {archived: int}. Archived notes hidden from default GET /notes list (add WHERE archived IS NOT TRUE filter). NoteModel: add archived (bool default False) column via db_init.py ALTER TABLE. FRONTEND: CollectionTree item: gear icon on hover (alongside the existing rename/delete). Gear click opens CollectionHealthPanel (Sheet or Dialog). Panel sections: Cohesion score pill (green >= 0.7, yellow >= 0.5, red < 0.5); Uncovered Notes list with 'Generate Flashcards' button (opens GenerateFlashcardsDialog pre-populated with those note_ids); Stale Notes list with 'Archive' button; Orphaned Notes count with 'Review' button (filters Notes tab to unassigned notes). STATES: Loading: skeleton sections. No data (< 6 notes): 'Not enough notes to compute cohesion' message. Cohesion unavailable: show dashes. TESTS: Unit test: cohesion_score of 6 identical vectors = 1.0; 6 random unit vectors < 0.5. Unit test: uncovered_notes excludes notes that have a matching deck card. Unit test: stale_notes selects only notes older than 90 days. Unit test: archive-stale sets archived=True and GET /notes excludes them by default.

stories.title: Export: collections as Obsidian-compatible Markdown vault and as Anki deck
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: Notes and flashcards are currently locked inside Luminary's SQLite database. Users who want to review outside the app (on mobile, in Obsidian, or in Anki) have no path. This story adds two export formats: (1) Obsidian-compatible Markdown vault -- one .md file per note, YAML frontmatter with tags and collection path, [[note_id|title]] links converted to [[note title]] wikilinks, exported as a .zip; (2) Anki deck -- one .apkg file containing all flashcards in a collection (or all note-sourced cards), using the standard Anki note model fields (Front, Back). USER JOURNEY: User opens CollectionTree -> right-click or actions menu on 'Quantum Mechanics' collection -> 'Export' menu with two options: 'Markdown Vault (.zip)' and 'Anki Deck (.apkg)' -> selects Markdown Vault -> browser downloads quantum-mechanics-vault.zip -> unzipping reveals .md files with YAML frontmatter and note content. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteLinkModel (S171) -- [[id|text]] markers in note content converted to Obsidian [[target title]] wikilinks. FlashcardModel (S93, S169) -- Anki export includes cards where deck=collection.name. NoteCollectionModel (S161) -- collection path used as Obsidian folder path in zip. BACKEND: ExportService in app/services/export_service.py. export_collection_markdown(collection_id, db) -> bytes: fetch all notes in collection (including child collections via NoteCollectionMemberModel join). For each note: build YAML frontmatter (tags, collection path as folder list, created_at, source document title if set). Convert note content: replace [[note_id|text]] markers with [[{target_note_preview[:40]}]] Obsidian wikilinks (resolve target by note_id lookup). Write each note as {note_id[:8]}-{slugified-first-line}.md. Zip all files with zipfile.ZipFile in memory. Return bytes. export_collection_anki(collection_id, db) -> bytes: use genanki library (add to pyproject.toml) to build a Deck with id=hash(collection_name), name=collection_name. For each FlashcardModel where deck=collection.name: create genanki.Note(fields=[flashcard.question, flashcard.answer]). Package as .apkg bytes. GET /collections/{id}/export?format=markdown: returns .zip with Content-Disposition attachment. GET /collections/{id}/export?format=anki: returns .apkg with Content-Disposition attachment. FRONTEND: CollectionTree item actions menu (three-dot or right-click): add 'Export' submenu with 'Markdown Vault' and 'Anki Deck' items. stories.title: Notes reader-first layout: widen content area, strategic actions, clickable tag navigation
stories.phase: V3 Phase 3 - UX Polish and Learner Focus
stories.description: CONTEXT: The current NoteEditorDialog and note viewer devote most vertical space to action buttons, tag inputs, and metadata fields. The note content itself is squeezed into a small scrollable area. This is backwards: a learner's primary action is to READ their note, not to manage it. This story redesigns the Notes tab note-viewing experience to put reading first. LAYOUT REDESIGN: When a note is opened (click row or 'Open' button), it opens in a full-width reader panel (or a Sheet/Drawer from the right) that occupies 70%+ of the available width. The full note content is displayed in a clean, readable typography block with no competing UI elements. A slim floating action bar (Edit, Tag, Link, Delete) appears at the bottom-right corner of the reader, visible but not dominant. Tag chips appear below the content with adequate spacing -- each chip is clickable and fires a 'luminary:navigate' DOM event routed to the Notes tab with that tag pre-applied as a filter, showing all notes with the same tag. The note title appears prominently at the top. Source document link (if any) is a small subtitle line below the title, clickable to navigate to the Learning tab and open that document. EDIT MODE: Clicking the Edit action button in-place transforms the reader panel into an edit mode (same layout, content area becomes a textarea, floating bar swaps to Save/Cancel). Tag management and collection assignment appear as expandable sections below the content in edit mode only. CLICKABLE TAGS: Each tag chip rendered anywhere in the Notes tab (list view, reader view, detail view) must be an anchor that dispatches luminary:navigate with { tab: 'notes', filter: { tag: tagPath } }. App.tsx must handle this and apply the tag filter in the Notes Zustand store. STATES: Loading note: content skeleton. No content: 'Start writing...'. Tag click with no matching notes: 'No other notes tagged with X'. TESTS: Unit test: tag click dispatches luminary:navigate with correct detail. Unit test: App.tsx luminary:navigate handler sets notes tag filter in Zustand store. Smoke: open a note, verify content fills the panel; click a tag, verify Notes list filters to that tag.

stories.title: Progress tab: replace Monitoring with learner-centric view, move dev metrics to admin route
stories.phase: V3 Phase 3 - UX Polish and Learner Focus
stories.description: CONTEXT: The Monitoring tab currently mixes two incompatible audiences: a learner who wants to know how they are progressing, and a developer who needs RAG pipeline health metrics. RAG quality metrics (HR@5, MRR, Faithfulness), Phoenix/Langfuse traces, and eval run logs have zero learning value for a student. They occupy significant screen real estate and create confusion. This story renames the Monitoring tab to 'Progress', strips all developer-centric panels from it, and moves them to a hidden /admin route accessible only via URL. The Progress tab becomes the learner's personal mastery dashboard. RENAME: Navigation tab 'Monitoring' -> 'Progress'. Tab component file renamed from Monitoring.tsx to Progress.tsx. LEARNER CONTENT (stays in Progress tab): Study activity chart (cards reviewed per day, streak), Overall mastery score across all decks, Cards due today vs mastered counts, Notes created over time chart, Documents ingested count, Time invested estimate. GOALS PANEL: GoalsPanel (currently in Study tab, S169) moves to Progress tab. Goals belong to the progress/mastery view, not the study workflow. Study tab no longer renders GoalsPanel. DEVELOPER CONTENT (moved to /admin route, not linked from nav): RagQualityPanel (HR@5, MRR, Faithfulness scores), PhoenixTracesPanel, LangfusePanel, EvalRunsPanel. Create a new AdminPage component rendered only at route /admin (not in the tab nav). Add a small hidden 'Dev Tools' link at the very bottom of the app footer (e.g. 'v3 | Dev') that navigates to /admin. BACKEND: No new endpoints required. GET /monitoring/* endpoints remain. The admin route simply re-uses existing API calls. STATES: Progress tab empty state (no study history): 'Start studying to see your progress here'. /admin route: no auth required (local app), just a plain page with all existing monitoring panels. TESTS: Unit test: GoalsPanel renders in Progress tab, not in Study tab. Unit test: /admin route renders RagQualityPanel. Smoke: GET /monitoring/rag-quality still returns 200 (endpoint unchanged).

stories.title: Context-aware flashcard generation: best-practices for non-fiction and technical books
stories.phase: V3 Phase 3 - UX Polish and Learner Focus
stories.description: CONTEXT: The current flashcard generator picks arbitrary text snippets and generates Q&A from them without verifying that the snippet contains a testable concept. This produces cards like 'What are the natural predators of wild boars?' when the document is a technology book that used wild boars as a passing analogy. The generator lacks the ability to distinguish between a core concept and an illustrative digression. This story rewrites the flashcard generation prompt and selection logic to follow evidence-based best practices. PRE-GENERATION FILTER: Before generating a question from a chunk, classify the chunk as 'concept', 'definition', 'example', 'analogy', 'narrative', or 'transition'. Only chunks classified as 'concept' or 'definition' are eligible for flashcard generation. 'example', 'analogy', and 'narrative' chunks are skipped unless they directly elaborate a concept chunk (adjacent in the section). PROMPT REDESIGN: System prompt for the LLM flashcard generator must include: (a) document genre hint (technical, narrative, academic -- inferred from document metadata or title keywords); (b) explicit instruction to generate questions that test understanding of a core idea, NOT surface recall of an illustrative example; (c) instruction to frame questions in terms of the concept being explained, with the answer explaining WHY or HOW, not just WHAT was mentioned. QUESTION QUALITY RULES (enforced in the system prompt): No questions whose answer is a proper noun from an analogy or story used to explain a concept. Questions must be answerable by someone who has NOT read the specific analogy but understands the underlying concept. Prefer 'Explain X in your own words' and 'What is the practical implication of Y' over 'According to the text, what does Z do'. DEDUPLICATION: If a generated Q&A pair has cosine similarity >= 0.85 with an existing card in the same deck (compare question embeddings), skip it. This extends the source_content_hash dedup from S174. BACKEND: Modify FlashcardService (or the relevant generation service) to add chunk classification step (single LLM call or heuristic keyword approach for performance), update the generation prompt with genre-aware instructions and quality rules, add embedding-based dedup check. New field: FlashcardModel.chunk_classification (nullable str) to record why a chunk was or was not selected. TESTS: Unit test: analogy-only chunks are skipped. Unit test: concept chunks produce questions framed around the concept, not the analogy. Unit test: near-duplicate card is skipped by embedding dedup. Integration test with DDIA-style tech text: generated cards mention 'replication', 'consistency', 'distributed systems' -- not 'wild boars'.

stories.title: Viz tab overhaul: collapsible View Options panel, select-all entity types, hide Call Graph
stories.phase: V3 Phase 3 - UX Polish and Learner Focus
stories.description: CONTEXT: The Viz tab exposes too many layer toggles as persistent buttons (Diagrams, Prerequisites, etc.), making the interface cluttered. The Call Graph mode is developer-centric and irrelevant for most learners. Entity type toggles (PERSON, ORGANIZATION, PLACE, CONCEPT, etc.) have no select-all or deselect-all control. The graph defaults to showing all entities from all documents, which can overwhelm a learner with thousands of nodes. This story redesigns the Viz tab controls for learner focus. CALL GRAPH: Move 'Call Graph' mode out of the top-level mode selector. It should only appear if the currently selected document contains code files. Auto-detect code documents by checking if document metadata includes file extensions (.py, .js, .ts, .go, etc.) or document type is 'code'. If no code document is selected, Call Graph does not appear in the mode list. If a code document IS selected, it appears as a secondary option within the Knowledge Graph settings panel. LAYER TOGGLES: All persistent layer toggle buttons (Diagrams, Prerequisites, and any others) are removed from the main toolbar. They are consolidated into a 'View Options' panel that is: (a) a collapsible sidebar section or (b) a dropdown panel triggered by a 'View Options' button. The View Options panel groups controls logically: Layers section (toggles for each layer type), Entity Types section (checkboxes for each GLiNER entity type with a 'Select All' / 'Deselect All' header). SELECT-ALL CONTROLS: Entity type filter section has two buttons: 'Select All' (checks all entity type checkboxes) and 'Deselect All' (unchecks all). Individual checkboxes remain. Empty graph after deselect all: 'Select at least one entity type to show the graph'. Cluster view with no clusters: falls back to standard node view. stories.title: YouTube transcript viewer: full feature parity with text documents in the Read section
stories.phase: V3 Phase 3 - UX Polish and Learner Focus
stories.description: CONTEXT: YouTube videos are ingested and their transcripts are stored in the document chunks table. However, the Read section (document reader) shows no content for YouTube documents -- users see an empty or 'No content' panel. A learner who ingests a lecture video expects to be able to read the transcript, search it, and take notes on it, just like any other document. This story adds transcript display to the Read tab and ensures full feature parity. TRANSCRIPT DISPLAY: When a YouTube document is opened in the Read section (Learning tab -> document list -> Read tab), the transcript is displayed as a sequence of chunked paragraphs with timestamps (if available). The transcript chunks are fetched from GET /documents/{id}/chunks (existing endpoint). Each chunk is rendered with its text and optionally its start_time (if stored in chunk metadata). Chunks are displayed in sequence order (chunk_index or unit_index). FEATURE PARITY: All existing Read tab features must work for YouTube documents: keyword search (highlights matching chunks), section navigation (uses heading or time-based sections), 'Take a Note' action (pre-fills NoteEditorDialog with the selected chunk as source content), 'Ask about this' button (sends chunk text to Chat with document context). If a feature relies on document file type checks, remove those guards and make the feature generic over all document types that have chunks. METADATA DISPLAY: Show video metadata at the top of the reader: video title, channel name, video URL (as a link), duration (if stored), ingestion date. FALLBACK: If no transcript chunks exist for the video (ingestion failed or in progress), show: 'Transcript is being processed. Check back shortly.' with a spinner that refreshes every 10 seconds. BACKEND: No new endpoints required. GET /documents/{id}/chunks must return chunks for YouTube documents (verify no document-type filter blocks this). GET /documents/{id} must return youtube_url and channel_name fields if stored. If these fields are not in DocumentModel, add them as nullable columns. TESTS: Unit test: GET /documents/{id}/chunks returns chunks for a document with source_type='youtube'. Unit test: DocumentModel includes youtube_url and channel_name nullable fields. Frontend smoke: open a YouTube document in Read tab, verify transcript paragraphs render.

stories.title: Auto-organize: guided plan with confirmation workflow
stories.phase: V3 Phase 4 - Learner Experience Refinement
stories.description: CONTEXT: Auto-organize runs HDBSCAN and shows individual suggestion cards one at a time. The user sees 'Suggestion 1: Notes Cluster (3 notes, 82% confidence)' and must accept or reject each individually. There is no holistic view of how all notes would be reorganized. USER JOURNEY: User clicks auto-organize wand -> clustering runs in background -> when complete, a full-page Organization Plan dialog opens -> header says 'Found 6 groups in 47 notes' -> each group shown as a card with editable name, note list with excerpts, confidence bar, include/exclude checkbox -> user renames a group, unchecks one, drags a note to another group -> clicks 'Apply Plan' -> all checked groups created as collections in one transaction -> dialog closes, Notes sidebar refreshes. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: S166 clustering service preserved. Existing individual accept/reject endpoints kept for backward compat. IMPLEMENTATION NOTES: Frontend: new OrganizationPlanDialog component. Fetch suggestions from GET /notes/cluster/suggestions, render as plan. Editable name via inline input. Drag notes between groups via dnd-kit or native drag. 'Apply Plan' calls new POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/batch-accept with [{suggestion_id, name_override}]. 'Dismiss' calls existing reject endpoint for each suggestion. Backend: add batch-accept endpoint in collections router. Single transaction: create NoteCollectionModel + NoteCollectionMemberModel rows for each accepted suggestion. Return list of created collection IDs.

POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/batch-accept creates all collections and memberships in a single database transaction; returns list of created collection IDs
'Dismiss' button closes dialog and marks all suggestions as rejected via existing reject endpoint

stories.title: Tag search: type-ahead search in Notes sidebar
stories.phase: V3 Phase 4 - Learner Experience Refinement
stories.description: CONTEXT: Tags in the Notes sidebar can only be browsed by expanding the tree and clicked to filter. There is no way to type a tag name and find it, especially problematic when the tag tree grows large with deep hierarchies. USER JOURNEY: User sees a 'Search tags...' input at the top of the TagTree sidebar -> types 'genetics' -> tree filters to show 'science / biology / genetics' with parents dimmed and 'genetics' highlighted bold -> presses Enter -> tag applied as note filter -> clears search -> full tree restored. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: None -- standalone frontend change. IMPLEMENTATION NOTES: Frontend-only change to TagTree.tsx. Add a controlled input at top. On change (debounced 150ms), filter the /tags/tree data client-side: keep tags where display_name or full slug includes query (case-insensitive). For each match, include parent chain with dimmed styling. Highlight matching substring with <mark> or bold span. Enter/click applies filterByTag from existing store. Clear restores full tree. No new API endpoints needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input rendered at the top of TagTree sidebar with placeholder 'Search tags...'
Typing filters the tag tree to show only tags matching the query (case-insensitive substring match on display_name or full slug)
Matching tags show their parent chain for context; parent tags are dimmed, matching tag is bold
Matching substring within the tag name is highlighted (bold or background color)
Pressing Enter or clicking a filtered tag applies it as the active tag filter (existing filterByTag behavior)
Clearing search input (backspace to empty or x button) restores the full unfiltered tag tree
Debounce of 150ms on search input to avoid excessive re-renders
Empty result: 'No tags matching [query]' message shown in tree area
Unit test: TagTree search input filters tags by substring match
Unit test: parent chain shown for deeply nested matching tags
